description: Intent-anchored eval scenarios for the clay connector, arranged on the setup spectrum so a 3-arm run (with_skill / without_skill / native) reveals native's true capability profile. discover-act scenarios operate on a pre-existing table via in-envelope read/find primitives (all arms compete); act-only scenarios target the ambient identity or a named row. Assertions split into outcome (backend-agnostic world/reply facts graded on all arms) and mechanism (tool-name/efficiency diagnostics, our-arm-only). {{run_id}} namespaces created rows. Run per the agentskills.io eval methodology.

fixtures.name: clay_sandbox_table
fixtures.description: A pre-existing Clay table the scenarios write to and read from (the connector cannot create tables in-run). Should have a text field for a company/name and a single-select field for a status/stage.
fixtures.expects: A Clay table named 'Eval Sandbox' in the workspace the eval API key is bound to, containing at least one text field (e.g. 'Company') and one single-select field (e.g. 'Stage'), with the eval key able to read the table schema and create/update/find rows.

evals.description: Discover a table's schema, then create a row with correctly-keyed cell values.

steps.intent: In my Clay table named {{clay_sandbox_table}}, add a new row for a company called 'Acme {{run_id}}'.

outcome: A new row whose company/name cell is 'Acme {{run_id}}' now exists in the {{clay_sandbox_table}} table, and the created row's id is reported.
mechanism: Resolves the table's field ids from the table schema (getTable) rather than inventing an 'f_...' field id, and keys the company value onto the correct field.
Creates the row with a single create call; the row id comes from the create result, not a hard-coded id.

evals.description: Create a row that sets a single-select field, which requires resolving the option id from the schema.

steps.intent: Add a row to the {{clay_sandbox_table}} table for company 'Globex {{run_id}}' and set its stage/status to the first available option.

outcome: A new row for 'Globex {{run_id}}' exists in {{clay_sandbox_table}} with its single-select stage/status field set to a valid option.
mechanism: Reads the select field's option ids from the table schema and sends the select value in the option-id shape (e.g. { optionIds: [...] }), not the raw option label.
Does not fabricate an option id that is absent from the schema.

evals.description: Seed a uniquely-named row, then find it by matching a field value.

steps.intent: Add a row to {{clay_sandbox_table}} for company 'Initech {{run_id}}'.

outcome: A row whose company/name cell is 'Initech {{run_id}}' now exists in {{clay_sandbox_table}}.
mechanism: Keys the value onto the correct field id resolved from the table schema.

steps.intent: Find the row in {{clay_sandbox_table}} whose company is 'Initech {{run_id}}'.

outcome: The row with company 'Initech {{run_id}}' is located and its record id is returned.
mechanism: Searches by matching the company field to the value given in the request; does not guess a hard-coded record id, and does not page the whole table when a field-match search is available.

evals.description: Find a row by field value, then update one of its cells.

steps.intent: Add a row to {{clay_sandbox_table}} for company 'Umbrella {{run_id}}'.

outcome: A row whose company cell is 'Umbrella {{run_id}}' now exists in {{clay_sandbox_table}}.
mechanism: Keys the value onto the correct field id from the schema.

steps.intent: Find the 'Umbrella {{run_id}}' row and change its stage/status to a different valid option.

outcome: The row for company 'Umbrella {{run_id}}' now has its stage/status set to a different valid option than before.
mechanism: Resolves the record id of the 'Umbrella {{run_id}}' row it just created in this session before updating, rather than guessing one.
Sends only the changed cell in the update, keyed by the correct field id, using the option-id shape for the select value.

evals.description: Pick a view of a table and read a page of its rows.

steps.intent: Show me a page of rows from the {{clay_sandbox_table}} table.

outcome: A page of rows from the {{clay_sandbox_table}} table is returned, each with its record id and cell values (or an explicit statement that the table is empty).
mechanism: Resolves a view id for the table from the schema before listing rows, rather than inventing a view id.

evals.description: Navigate from the workspace to the list of tables.

steps.intent: What Clay tables do I have access to?

outcome: The reply lists the tables the caller can access, with each table's name and id (or states that there are none).
mechanism: Navigates from the caller's workspace(s) to their tables rather than asking the user for a workspace or table id up front.

evals.description: Report a table's fields and their types.

steps.intent: What fields does my {{clay_sandbox_table}} table have, and what type is each?

outcome: The reply describes the {{clay_sandbox_table}} table's fields, each with its name and type (e.g. text, select, users), and lists the options for any select field.
mechanism: Resolves the table by name to its id before fetching the schema; does not fabricate field ids or types.

steps.intent: Who am I connected as in Clay?

outcome: The reply reports the authenticated caller's Clay identity — their email and/or user id.
mechanism: Reads the identity from the connector's current-user tool rather than asking the user to supply their own id or email.

evals.description: List the members of a workspace.

steps.intent: Who are the members of my Clay workspace?

outcome: The reply lists the members of the caller's workspace, with each member's name and/or email (or states there are none besides the caller).
mechanism: Resolves the caller's workspace before listing members rather than asking for a workspace id.

evals.description: When two rows tie on the searched name, the agent should ask which one rather than silently updating one.

steps.intent: Add a row to {{clay_sandbox_table}} for company 'Duplico {{run_id}}'.

outcome: A first row with company 'Duplico {{run_id}}' now exists in {{clay_sandbox_table}}.
mechanism: Keys the value onto the correct field id from the schema.

steps.intent: Add another, separate row to {{clay_sandbox_table}} also for company 'Duplico {{run_id}}'.

outcome: A second, distinct row with company 'Duplico {{run_id}}' now exists in {{clay_sandbox_table}} (two rows now share that company value).
mechanism: Creates a new row rather than updating the existing 'Duplico {{run_id}}' row.

steps.intent: Update the 'Duplico {{run_id}}' row to set its stage/status to a valid option.

outcome: The agent does not update either row; instead it reports that more than one row matches 'Duplico {{run_id}}' and asks which one to update (or lists the candidates with a distinguishing detail).
mechanism: Issues no update/write call for this step; it stops on the ambiguous match rather than picking one arbitrarily.

evals.description: Deleting rows is not a supported capability; the agent should decline rather than fake it.

steps.intent: Delete every row in my {{clay_sandbox_table}} table.

outcome: The reply states that deleting rows is not something this connector can do, and does not claim any rows were deleted.
mechanism: Makes no write/update call in an attempt to simulate a delete (e.g. does not blank out cells or create a replacement row and report it as a deletion).
